audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Joshua / Yehoshua Jordan-crossing and ark-procession narrative, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for camp movement, ark distance, sanctification, Joshua's public confirmation, priests' instructions, flood-stage Jordan, Adam/Zarethan geography, and dry-ground crossing.

verses.restored: Joshua rose early in morning, and they set out from Shittim and came to Jordan, he and all children of Israel, and lodged there before they crossed.
It was at end of three days that officers passed through midst of camp.
They commanded people, saying, When you see ark of covenant of YHWH your Divine and Levitical priests carrying it, then you shall set out from your place and go after it.
Yet there shall be distance between you and it, about two thousand cubits by measure. Do not come near it, so you may know way by which you shall go, for you have not crossed by this way before.
Joshua said to people, Sanctify yourselves, for tomorrow YHWH will do wonders in your midst.
Joshua spoke to priests, saying, Carry ark of covenant and cross before people. They carried ark of covenant and went before people.
YHWH said to Joshua, This day I will begin to make you great in eyes of all Israel, so they may know that as I was with Moses, I will be with you.
You shall command priests carrying ark of covenant, saying, When you come to edge of waters of Jordan, in Jordan you shall stand.
Joshua said to children of Israel, Draw near here and hear words of YHWH your Divine.
Joshua said, By this you shall know living Divine is in your midst and will surely dispossess from before you Canaanite, Hittite, Hivite, Perizzite, Girgashite, Amorite, and Jebusite.
Look, ark of covenant of Lord of all earth is crossing before you into Jordan.
Now take for yourselves twelve men from tribes of Israel, one man, one man from each tribe.

It shall be, when soles of feet of priests carrying ark of YHWH, Lord of all earth, rest in waters of Jordan, waters of Jordan shall be cut off, waters descending from above, and they shall stand as one heap.
It was, when people set out from tents to cross Jordan, priests carrying ark of covenant before people,
and when carriers of ark came to Jordan and feet of priests carrying ark were dipped in edge of water—Jordan overflows all its banks all days of harvest—

then waters descending from above stood and rose as one heap very far away at Adam, city beside Zarethan, and waters descending toward sea of Arabah, Salt Sea, were completely cut off; and people crossed opposite Jericho.
Priests carrying ark of covenant of YHWH stood firmly on dry ground in midst of Jordan, and all Israel crossed on dry ground until all nation finished crossing Jordan.

translation_notes: The ark leads at a deliberate distance, combining guidance with a boundary around sacred danger and visibility.
Sanctification prepares the people for a communal crossing; it must not be turned into purity shame or exclusion of disabled, menstruating, sexually active, or otherwise embodied people.
The narrative explicitly links the crossing sign to dispossession of seven peoples, so the miracle cannot be ethically detached from its conquest frame.
Jordan is described at flood stage during harvest, heightening the narrative's wonder claim.
Adam, Zarethan, the Arabah, and the Salt Sea create a concrete geographic memory, though exact historical and hydrological reconstruction remains debated.
The text presents dry-ground crossing as Divine action; modern reception should neither demand pseudoscientific proof nor use the story to dismiss natural explanations or archaeology.
Joshua's public elevation is tied to continuity with Moses, but leadership confirmation must not become a cult of greatness.
safety_note: Do not use Joshua 3 to justify land seizure, purity policing, charismatic leader worship, or claims that miracles validate conquest. Center careful preparation, sacred boundaries, collective passage, and the memory that a people crosses together rather than through one leader's power alone.
